component.name: Tag
component.description: Labels UI objects for quick recognition and navigation — categories, statuses, filters, or metadata. Compact, non-interactive by default; optionally removable or linked.
component.note: Sharp rectangle, 1px per-appearance border, Inter semibold 12px/16px, 20px tall. 'green' maps to the teal palette. Hover/pressed are CSS pseudo-states and only apply when interactive. Synced to Figma 'Tag'.

shape.paddingInline: spacing.2xs (4px); removable trims right to spacing.3xs (2px)
shape.gap: spacing.xs (6px) between text and remove button

appearances.gray: bg neutral.100 (hover .200 / press .300), border neutral.300, text neutral.600
appearances.green: bg teal.100 (hover .200 / press .300), border teal.500, text teal.800 (darkened from .700 for AA)
appearances.red: bg danger.50 (hover .100 / press .200), border danger.400, text danger.700
appearances.purple: bg purple.50 (hover .100 / press .200), border purple.400, text purple.700
appearances.blue: bg blue.50 (hover .100 / press .200), border blue.400, text blue.700
appearances.orange: bg orange.50 (hover .100 / press .200), border orange.400, text orange.700

tokens.removeButton: 16px, inherits tag text color; hover/press tint with the appearance bg steps

useCases: Label a category, topic, or content type
Show a status or attribute (e.g. Draft, Published)
Display applied filters as removable chips
Surface metadata keywords on a card or row

commonPatterns.name: Status label
commonPatterns.description: A colored, non-interactive tag conveying state.

commonPatterns.name: Removable filter chip
commonPatterns.description: Applied filters the user can dismiss; onRemove updates the filter set.

commonPatterns.name: Linked category
commonPatterns.description: Tag that navigates to a filtered/category view.

commonPatterns.name: Tag list
commonPatterns.description: A wrapping row of tags on a card or detail header.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a tag as a primary action button
antiPatterns.reason: Tags are labels; their low visual weight under-communicates an action.
Color is not perceivable by all users; appearance should reinforce, not replace, the label text.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep a clear text label; use appearance as secondary emphasis.

antiPatterns.reason: Tags are single-line and compact; long text breaks the rhythm.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep to 1–2 words; use body text for longer content.

antiPatterns.scenario: Making a tag both a link and removable in the same chip
antiPatterns.reason: Two competing targets in one small control are error-prone and ambiguous.
antiPatterns.alternative: Pick one — link OR removable — per tag.

slots.remove: Optional — trailing × button rendered when onRemove is set

interactions.linkHover: Whole chip darkens to the appearance hover/press bg step; text underlines
interactions.remove: Remove button tints with the appearance bg steps on hover/press and calls onRemove
interactions.focus: 2px forest.700 outline on the interactive element (link or remove button)

accessibility.role: Static tag is plain text; link → link role; remove → button with aria-label (default 'Remove')
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Interactive tags are tabbable; Enter/Space activate the link or remove button
accessibility.screenReader: Remove button should ideally name the tag it removes (pass removeLabel like 'Remove Sports')
accessibility.contrastNote: Each appearance pairs a dark .700 text on a light .50/.100 fill, meeting AA for text; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning.

athenaConstraints.decorativePalettes: Tags consume the teal/purple/blue/orange/danger/neutral primitives directly (no semantic alias layer for the six tones)

figmaDivergences.implementation: real CSS pseudo-states, applied only to interactive tags (link or remove button)
figmaDivergences.reason: A static label tag has no interaction; matches the rest of the system.
Figma's teal.700 on teal.100 is 4.03:1, failing WCAG AA for 12px text. teal.800 is ~4.9:1. Same AA-first approach as Button's danger divergence.

aiHints.context: Reach for Tag to label or categorize content, show a status, or present removable filters. Keep text to 1–2 words. Use onRemove for filter chips, href for category links. For actions use Button; for counts/notifications a dedicated Badge would be preferable (not yet in the system).
neverUseWhen: You need an action trigger — use Button
Meaning depends on color with no text — add a clear label
The content is a sentence — use body text
You need both a link and a remove action in one chip — split into two
